title: Classification of phi on S_{n,l,b}: joint (inv, blocks) bookkeeping

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma G3 (technical core).** Prove the four-part classification lemma for the Sagan–Swanson involution $\phi$ acting on
$$S_{n,\ell,b}=\bigcup_{c=0}^b\{w\in\OSP(n,n-c)\ :\ \inv(w)=\ell-b-c\}.$$

Establish first the standing window: $w\in S_{n,\ell,b}\Rightarrow \ell-2b\le\inv(w)\le\ell-b$ and $n-b\le\blocks(w)\le n$ (from $0\le c\le b$).

Then prove:
- **(i)** If $w$ is type I (mergeable) with $\inv(w)=\ell-2c$ then $c=b$, and $\phi$ lowers $\inv$ by $1$ to $\ell-2b-1$, so $\phi(w)\notin S_{n,\ell,b}$.
- **(ii)** Otherwise-type-I $w$ has $c<b$; then $\inv(\phi(w))=\ell-b-c-1\ge\ell-2b$ and $\phi(w)\in S_{n,\ell,b}$.
- **(iii)** Type II (splittable) $w$ has $\blocks(w)<n$ so $c>0$, hence $\inv(w)<\ell-b$, so $\inv(\phi(w))\le\ell-b$ and $\phi(w)\in S_{n,\ell,b}$.
- **(iv)** Type III is impossible: it forces $c=0,\ \ell=b$, contradicting $b\le\lfloor(\ell-1)/2\rfloor$ (using $\ell\ge1$).

**Genuine subtlety to address in full.** $\phi$ changes **both** $\inv$ and $\blocks$ simultaneously (merge: $-1$ block, $-1$ inv; split: $+1$ block, $+1$ inv). The target set $S_{n,\ell,b}$ is a *union over strata* $\OSP(n,n-c)$. The proof must verify that the image $\phi(w)$ lands in the *correct* stratum $\OSP(n,n-c')$ with $0\le c'\le b$ (not merely that $\inv$ stays in range). The current text checks $\inv$ ranges but the joint membership-after-$\phi$ across strata deserves explicit treatment, since $\inv(\phi(w))=\ell-b-c'$ must hold for the *new* block count $c'=c\pm1$.

**Status:** the resulting pairing has been verified numerically ($n\le6$). The gap is a fully rigorous joint $(\inv,\blocks)$ argument.
